data.headline: 'Don't play us': US VP Vance warns Iran as he heads to Pakistan for talks
data.summary: US Vice President JD Vance warned Iran as he headed to Pakistan for talks. Vance emphasized the need for Iran to negotiate in good faith. The US will extend an open hand if Iran negotiates sincerely. The warning comes amid weeks of Middle East conflict. Vance's visit to Pakistan aims to lead crucial negotiations to end the conflict.

data.headline: 'Grave consequences': Kharge says Centre's women quota move violates poll code
data.summary: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge expressed concerns over the government's proposed women's reservation law amendments. He alleged that the move violates the Model Code of Conduct. Kharge questioned the timing of the special Parliament session, citing potential political motives. The Congress party emphasized the need for deep deliberation on delimitation and women's empowerment. The opposition unity was asserted by Kharge in response to the government's proposed law amendments.

data.headline: Pluto to become 9th planet again? Nasa chief says 'looking into it', replies to 10-yr-old's letter
data.summary: NASA's chief has responded to a 10-year-old's letter about Pluto's status as a planet. The child had written to NASA asking them to reconsider Pluto's classification. NASA is looking into the matter, according to the chief's response. Pluto was previously considered the ninth planet in our solar system but was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006. The decision to reclassify Pluto was made by the International Astronomical Union.

data.headline: LSG or GT? Sara Tendulkar's reaction to pap's question goes viral - Watch
data.summary: Sara Tendulkar's reaction to a question about her favorite team has gone viral. She was asked which team she supports ahead of the LSG vs GT match. Sara smiled and stayed silent, possibly due to her brother Arjun Tendulkar playing for LSG. Arjun Tendulkar is a young all-rounder and the son of Sachin Tendulkar. He has yet to get many chances but is considered a promising talent this IPL season.

data.headline: Watch: India unleashes Apache's fury; Army's deadliest chopper dominates Brahmastra
data.summary: India's Army unveiled its AH-64E Apache attack helicopters at Exercise Brahmastra. The Apache helicopters demonstrated precision strikes and coordination with ground forces. The Apaches are now deployed in Jodhpur as a key asset for national security. The helicopters showcased their advanced combat capabilities during the exercise. The deployment of Apaches highlights their role in modern warfare for India's national security.

data.headline: Scientists discover a fungus in Western Australia that eats gold and want to take it into space
data.summary: Scientists have discovered a fungus in Western Australia that can absorb and break down gold. The fungus, which is yet to be named, was found to have the ability to dissolve gold into a liquid form. Researchers are interested in taking this fungus to space to see if it can help in extracting gold from asteroids. This discovery could potentially lead to new methods of gold extraction and have significant implications for the mining industry. The fungus's ability to break down gold could also have applications in fields such as electronics and catalysis.

data.headline: Sadalge Studio Gallery hosts terracotta workshop in Belagavi
data.summary: The Sadalge Studio Gallery hosted a terracotta workshop in Belagavi. The workshop aimed to promote the art of terracotta and provide a platform for artists to showcase their skills. Participants learned various techniques of terracotta art and created their own pieces during the workshop. The event was well-received by the local community and art enthusiasts. The workshop contributed to the preservation and promotion of traditional Indian art forms.

data.headline: Jharkhand BJP seeks CBI or judicial probe into ₹21 crore treasury scam
data.summary: The Jharkhand BJP has demanded a CBI or judicial probe into a treasury scam. The scam involves alleged irregular salary withdrawals from police treasuries in Hazaribagh and Bokaro. The total amount involved in the scam is ₹21 crore. The State Finance Department has ordered stricter verification of salary bills to prevent such incidents in the future. The BJP's demand for a probe is aimed at ensuring accountability and transparency in the state's financial dealings.

data.headline: Government orders 60-day age relaxation for the admission of Class 1
data.summary: The government has ordered a 60-day age relaxation for Class 1 admissions. This decision aims to help students who were unable to enroll due to the pandemic. The age relaxation will provide an opportunity for young students to join Class 1. The order is expected to benefit students across the country. The government's move is seen as a relief measure for parents and students affected by the pandemic.

data.headline: Special train services between Mysuru and Jalpaiguri to clear summer rush
data.summary: The special train services will run between Mysuru and Jalpaiguri to clear the summer rush. This move aims to provide relief to passengers during the peak travel season. The train services will help in managing the increased demand for tickets during summer. The introduction of these special trains will also help in reducing waitlists and providing more travel options to passengers.

data.headline: Upgraded HAL Heritage Centre and Aerospace Museum to reopen on April 13
data.summary: The HAL Heritage Centre and Aerospace Museum is set to reopen on April 13. The museum features state-of-the-art installations, including a life-size replica of the Hindustan Turbo Fan Engine. A 3D anamorphic runway showcasing HAL's iconic aircraft, such as Prachand and Tejas, is also part of the museum. The upgraded museum aims to provide an immersive experience for visitors. The reopening of the museum is expected to attract aviation enthusiasts and showcase India's advancements in the aerospace sector.

data.headline: Training on aeroponic saffron cultivation
data.summary: The training on aeroponic saffron cultivation aims to promote this innovative farming method. Aeroponic cultivation involves growing plants in the air without soil, using a nutrient-rich mist. This technique can increase saffron yields and reduce water consumption. The training is expected to benefit farmers by introducing them to modern and sustainable farming practices. By adopting aeroponic saffron cultivation, farmers can improve their livelihoods and contribute to the country's agricultural development.

data.headline: Krishna district child marriage case registered after wedding photos surface online
data.summary: A child marriage case has been registered in Krishna district. The marriage came to light after wedding photos were uploaded on social media. A family member had shared the photos in a social media group, alerting officials to the incident. The case was subsequently registered, with further investigation underway. Authorities have taken action after the wedding photos surfaced online, highlighting the role of social media in combating such crimes.

data.headline: Two arrested for murdering doctor
data.summary: Two individuals have been arrested for allegedly murdering a doctor. The incident has led to a police investigation, with the suspects being taken into custody. The murder is believed to have occurred due to a personal dispute, but the exact motives are still being determined. The police are currently gathering evidence and interviewing witnesses to build a case against the accused. The arrested individuals will face trial for their alleged role in the doctor's murder.

data.headline: Some minority leaders conspired to defeat Cong: MLC
data.summary: A Member of the Legislative Council (MLC) made a statement about some minority leaders. The statement claimed that these leaders conspired to defeat the Congress. This allegation has significant implications for the political landscape. The MLC's statement may impact the relationships between different political parties and minority groups. The claim is likely to spark further discussion and debate in the political arena.

data.headline: Pakistan terrorist tasked Dehradun man with filming key locations for attack: Special Task Force
data.summary: A Pakistan-based terrorist allegedly tasked a man from Dehradun with filming key locations. The man was instructed to capture footage for a potential attack. The Special Task Force is investigating the matter. The incident highlights the threat of cross-border terrorism in India. Key locations in Dehradun were potentially being targeted for a terrorist attack.

data.headline: Kerala polls: BJP Christian leaders say ‘ditched’ by Church over alleged UDF backing amid FCRA row
data.summary: Kerala polls have seen a controversy involving BJP Christian leaders. They claim to have been ditched by the Church due to alleged UDF backing. The issue is linked to the FCRA row, which affects foreign funding for certain organizations. The Church's stance has led to tensions within the BJP's Christian faction. The incident highlights the complex political dynamics at play in Kerala's elections.

data.headline: Acquitted in Malegaon blast case, Col Purohit  promoted to Brigadier, to serve for 2 more years
data.summary: Col Purohit was acquitted in the Malegaon blast case. He has been promoted to the rank of Brigadier. Col Purohit will serve for 2 more years. The promotion is based on his seniority and experience. The Malegaon blast case is a significant incident in India's history of terrorism.

data.headline: B.Tech student’s death at Bengaluru university: Father raises questions, Jharkhand minister steps in
data.summary: A B.Tech student's death at a Bengaluru university has raised questions. The student's father is seeking answers regarding the circumstances of the death. A Jharkhand minister has stepped in to investigate the matter. The incident has sparked concerns about student safety and well-being at the university. An investigation is currently underway to determine the cause of the student's death.

data.headline: RBI proposes to include PSUs in upper-layer NBFCs
data.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has proposed to include Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) in upper-layer Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs). The draft amendment directions specify that upper-layer NBFCs will be those with assets over Rs 1 lakh crore. This move is part of the Reserve Bank of India's scale-based regulation framework. The proposed change aims to strengthen regulatory oversight of large NBFCs. The amendment directions are subject to further review and consultation before being finalized.

data.headline: West Asia crisis: Government plans weekly monitoring of EXIM flows
data.summary: India's commerce ministry will launch a weekly system to monitor export-import trends and industry stress. The move addresses concerns over supply chain disruptions and rising costs impacting sectors like apparel and medical devices. Discussions also focused on packaging material challenges and the need for critical input availability. Ports are directed to enhance transparency and efficiency to support trade. The weekly monitoring system aims to mitigate the impact of the West Asia crisis on India's trade.

data.headline: India wants manufacturing at 25% of GDP — will AI in factories help?
data.summary: India aims to increase the manufacturing sector's contribution to GDP to 25%. The government is exploring ways to achieve this goal, including the adoption of artificial intelligence in factories. The use of AI in manufacturing can improve efficiency, productivity, and competitiveness. The implementation of AI in factories can also create new job opportunities in fields like data analysis and machine learning. The integration of AI in manufacturing is expected to play a crucial role in helping India achieve its economic growth targets.

data.headline: Mathura tragedy: 6 dead, several missing after tourist boat capsizes in Yamuna; rescue ops on
data.summary: A boat carrying devotees capsized in the Yamuna River near Kesi Ghat in Mathura. At least six people lost their lives in the incident. The boat was one of two carrying around 30 individuals when it suddenly sank on Friday afternoon. Rescue operations are currently underway to locate those still missing. Several people have already been brought to safety.

data.headline: After polls, police to continue with intensified vigil in Kozhikode
data.summary: The police in Kozhikode will continue with intensified vigil after the polls. The squads will stay alert at the district and State-level borders. Vehicle-checking will be carried out in assigned areas. This move is aimed at maintaining law and order in the region. The police will remain vigilant to prevent any potential disturbances.

data.headline: Hyderabad police launches ‘She Netra’ teams to crackdown on hidden cameras; provides detection kits
data.summary: Hyderabad police has launched 'She Netra' teams to crack down on hidden cameras. The teams will detect and prevent the installation of hidden cameras in public places. Detection kits have been provided to the teams to aid in their efforts. Individuals or establishments found with hidden cameras will face strict criminal action. The initiative aims to enhance women's safety in the city.

data.headline: Bihar Assembly receives bomb threat, intensive search underway: Police
data.summary: The Bihar Assembly has received a bomb threat. An intensive search is currently underway. Bomb and dog squad teams have been deployed to ensure the area is secure. The police are taking all necessary precautions to handle the situation. The search operation is ongoing to determine the credibility of the threat.

data.headline: Approach authorities instead of rushing to court, Supreme Court tells petitioner who filed 25 PILs
data.summary: The Supreme Court advised a petitioner to approach authorities instead of rushing to court. The petitioner had filed 25 Public Interest Litigations (PILs) seeking various directions. The PILs included framing a policy for a common link language and drafting a policy for a legal awareness show on television. The Supreme Court's suggestion indicates a preference for exhausting alternative remedies before seeking judicial intervention. The case highlights the importance of responsible litigation and the role of courts in promoting effective governance.

data.headline: Husband sentenced to triple life term for murder of woman civil police officer in Kannur
data.summary: A husband has been sentenced to a triple life term for the murder of a woman civil police officer in Kannur. The incident occurred in November 2024. The victim had earlier filed a case in the Kannur family court expressing her unwillingness to continue the marriage. The court's verdict has been handed down in the case, with the husband receiving a severe sentence. The case highlights the issue of domestic violence and the importance of addressing marital disputes.

data.headline: Govt's proposed delimitation exercise to have 'grave consequences': Kharge in CWC meet
data.summary: The Congress chief, Kharge, expressed concerns over the government's proposed delimitation exercise. He stated that it would have grave consequences. Kharge emphasized the need for a collective strategy with other Opposition parties. The Congress party plans to move forward unitedly on this issue. The delimitation exercise is expected to have significant implications for the country's political landscape.

data.headline: Justice Varma's resignation welcome, should have done it earlier, says HC bar association president
data.summary: Allahabad High Court judge Varma has resigned. He was facing impeachment proceedings due to burnt currency notes found at his residence. The resignation was submitted to President Droupadi Murmu. The HC bar association president welcomed the resignation, stating it should have been done earlier. The incident led to a significant development in the Indian judiciary.

data.headline: ‘Shocks our conscience’: Supreme Court summons Ghaziabad police chief over 4-year-old’s rape-murder
data.summary: The Supreme Court has summoned the Ghaziabad police chief over the rape and murder of a 4-year-old girl. The court's decision was made in response to a petition seeking justice for the victim. The incident has sparked widespread outrage and calls for stricter laws against child abuse. The Supreme Court's intervention highlights the need for prompt action in such heinous crimes. The case is being closely monitored by the court to ensure that justice is served.

data.headline: Bengaluru’s Kempegowda International Airport to handle 80 million passengers annually by 2030: Murlidhar Mohol
data.summary: Bengaluru's Kempegowda International Airport is expected to handle 80 million passengers annually by 2030. This projection was made by Murlidhar Mohol. The airport's expansion plans are underway to accommodate the growing passenger traffic. The airport currently handles a significant number of domestic and international flights. The projected growth in passenger traffic is likely to boost the city's economy and infrastructure development.

data.headline: High-tech ISI-BKI ‘espionage’ network targeting Delhi defence hubs busted; 11 nabbed from Delhi, Punjab
data.summary: A high-tech espionage network allegedly run by ISI and BKI has been busted in Delhi. The network was targeting defence hubs in the national capital. Eleven people have been arrested from Delhi and Punjab in connection with the espionage ring. The arrests were made following a joint operation by the Delhi Police and the Punjab Police. The espionage network was using advanced technology to gather sensitive information about India's defence establishments.

data.headline: India’s forex reserves up by $9.06 billion to $697.12 billion in week ending April 3
data.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves increased by $9.06 billion to $697.12 billion in the week ending April 3. The rise in reserves brings the total to nearly $700 billion. This increase follows a period of decline in the country's foreign exchange reserves. The Reserve Bank of India has been actively managing the currency, contributing to the stabilization of the country's financial position. The significant increase in forex reserves indicates a positive trend in India's economic stability.

data.headline: Nitin Gadkari bats for 100% land acquisition before approving national highway projects
data.summary: Union Minister Nitin Gadkari proposed that new national highway projects should only be approved with complete land acquisition. He stated that land acquisition and clearances are significant challenges in road construction. Gadkari indicated that highway assets worth Rs 15 lakh crore are available for monetisation. The proposal aims to streamline the process of national highway project approvals. This move is expected to reduce delays and facilitate smoother execution of road construction projects.

data.headline: DGFT rolls out procedure for allocation of calcined coke
data.summary: The Directorate General of Foreign Trade has introduced new rules for importing calcined petroleum coke for aluminium production. Applications for allocations are now open for the 2026-27 period. A certificate of inspection for rice exports is required for specific European countries. Exports to other European nations are exempt from this requirement for six months. The new rules aim to streamline the import and export process for these commodities.

data.headline: Iran-US truce talks: Pakistan is mere venue, China is the real mediator
data.summary: Iran and the US are engaging in truce talks. Pakistan is serving as the venue for these discussions. However, China is reportedly playing a key role as the mediator in these negotiations. The involvement of China indicates a broader geopolitical dynamic at play. The talks are significant as they may impact regional and global stability.

data.headline: Spy ring busted, 10 held: How Pak was keeping real-time eye on Delhi's critical areas
data.summary: Delhi Police have dismantled a Pakistan ISI-backed spy ring, arresting 10 operatives for surveilling key locations. The operatives installed cameras to send real-time data to their handlers. This intelligence was intended to plan future terror attacks in India. The operation followed weeks of surveillance by the authorities. Authorities are analyzing seized devices to find more links to the spy ring.

data.headline: RR vs RCB: Sooryavanshi vs Bhuvneshwar, Kohli vs Sandeep - 5 key battles in today's match
data.summary: Rajasthan Royals face Royal Challengers Bengaluru in an IPL match. Both teams aim to extend their unbeaten streaks. Key player battles will define the encounter at Barsapara Cricket Stadium. Virat Kohli vs Sandeep Sharma is one of the key battles. The match is a high-stakes encounter between the table-toppers and the defending champions.

data.headline: Sensex up over 900 points - top reasons for market rally
data.summary: The Indian equity benchmarks, Sensex and Nifty50, rallied in trade on Friday. Sensex was up over 900 points, crossing the 77,500 mark. Nifty50 went above 17,000 is incorrect as per the text, it went above 24,000 is also incorrect as the text does not provide the previous day's value, however, Nifty50 did go above 24,000 on Friday. The market rally came after a day of losses on Thursday. The exact reasons for the market rally are not specified in the given text.

data.headline: Strait of Hormuz: Open or shut? What ceasefire means — FAQs answered
data.summary: A fragile ceasefire has eased immediate escalation risks in the Strait of Hormuz. Shipping remains constrained and conditional despite limited vessel movement resuming. The ceasefire has not returned shipping to normal due to ongoing security considerations. Global energy markets remain sensitive to any disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. The situation is being closely monitored due to its potential impact on international trade and energy supplies.

data.headline: 'Dhurandhar 2' collection: Ranveer's film eyes Rs 1,050 crore
data.summary: Ranveer Singh's spy thriller 'Dhurandhar 2' has crossed the Rs 1000 crore mark in its fourth week. The film is holding strong despite the IPL season and is eyeing Rs 1050 crore. The movie's gripping narrative of an R&AW agent's infiltration has captivated audiences. The film's impressive box office success is driven by its engaging storyline. 'Dhurandhar 2' continues its phenomenal run, setting new benchmarks in the Indian film industry.

data.headline: SC steps in on plea of Indians ‘stuck’ in Russia, ‘forced’ into war with Ukraine
data.summary: The Supreme Court has intervened in a plea from Indians stuck in Russia and forced into war with Ukraine. These Indians claim they are fighting for a foreign state unwillingly and are in an extreme situation. They fear being dead or injured due to the ongoing conflict. The plea highlights the plight of Indian citizens trapped in the Russia-Ukraine war. The Supreme Court's intervention may provide relief to these stranded Indians.

data.headline: Morning vs. evening: how do the benefits of yoga differ depending on when you do it
data.summary: A new study examined the benefits of yoga at different times of day. Participants who practiced yoga in the morning reported improved sleep and a preference for early-day activity. This shift towards 'morningness' aligns with natural biological rhythms. The study found a greater reduction in sleep disturbances among morning yoga practitioners. Overall, the timing of yoga practice can impact its benefits, particularly for sleep and daily activity preferences.

data.headline: Supreme Court refuses to entertain bail plea of suspended Punjab Police DIG in corruption case
data.summary: The Supreme Court has refused to entertain the bail plea of a suspended Punjab Police DIG. The DIG is accused in a corruption case. The high court had earlier dismissed his bail plea, allowing him to reapply after key witnesses are examined. The case involves corruption allegations against the suspended police officer. The Supreme Court's decision upholds the high court's ruling, denying the DIG's bail plea at this stage.

data.headline: Vijay’s ‘Jana Nayagan’ leaked online: KVN Productions initiate probe
data.summary: Vijay's movie 'Jana Nayagan' has been leaked online. The production team, KVN Productions, is taking action against the leak. Led by Venkat K Narayana, the team is preparing to take legal action. The leak is being treated as a case of digital piracy. KVN Productions will investigate and prosecute those responsible for the leak.

data.headline: Gaganyaan mission: ISRO completes second Integrated Air Drop Test
data.summary: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has completed the second Integrated Air Drop Test (IADT-02) for the Gaganyaan mission. Union Minister of State for Science and Technology Dr. Jitendra Singh announced the accomplishment. The test is a crucial step in the development of the Gaganyaan mission, which aims to send Indian astronauts to space. The IADT-02 test demonstrates the capability to recover the crew module after it is dropped from an altitude. The successful completion of the test brings India closer to achieving its human spaceflight mission goals.

data.headline: Government extends Minimum Import Price for natural honey
data.summary: The government has extended the Minimum Import Price for natural honey. This move is expected to impact the import and export dynamics of the honey industry. An additional condition has been added for exports of feathers, skins, and other parts, as per a notification by the DGFT. The Minimum Import Price is a trade policy tool used to protect domestic industries from cheap imports. The extension of the Minimum Import Price for natural honey aims to promote domestic production and reduce dependence on imports.

data.headline: 42 Maoist cadres surrender with arms and gold before Telangana police
data.summary: 42 Maoist cadres have surrendered before the Telangana police, bringing with them arms and gold. This surrender marks a significant development in the state's efforts to combat left-wing extremism. According to the Director General of Police (DGP), there are no active armed formations of the CPI (Maoist) remaining in Telangana. The surrender is seen as a major achievement for the Telangana police, indicating a decline in Maoist activity in the region. The development is expected to have implications for the state's internal security and counter-insurgency efforts.

data.headline: Kerala filmmaker Ranjith gets bail in sexual harassment case
data.summary: Kerala filmmaker Ranjith has been granted bail in a sexual harassment case. The court has set certain conditions for his bail, including surrendering his passport. Ranjith must remain within the Ernakulam district as part of his bail conditions. He is also prohibited from entering Fort Kochi, where the alleged offence occurred during a film shoot. The bail conditions are in place to ensure Ranjith does not interfere with the investigation or leave the country.

data.headline: Strong El Niño to emerge in Pacific Ocean after July, says NOAA
data.summary: A strong El Niño event is predicted to emerge in the Pacific Ocean after July, according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA). El Niño events occur when the surface temperature of the Pacific Ocean warms up more than usual, leading to significant changes in weather patterns. This event is expected to have widespread implications for global climate patterns, including droughts, floods, and heatwaves. The NOAA prediction is based on climate models and oceanic and atmospheric data, which indicate a high likelihood of a strong El Niño event. The emergence of a strong El Niño event can have significant impacts on agriculture, water resources, and human settlements, particularly in vulnerable regions.

data.headline: India ‘deeply concerned’ by civilian casualties in Lebanon amid US-Iran ceasefire
data.summary: India has expressed deep concern over civilian casualties in Lebanon. The concern arises amid a ceasefire between the US and Iran. The situation in Lebanon is being closely monitored by India. The ceasefire between the US and Iran is expected to ease tensions in the region. India's statement highlights its commitment to peace and stability in the Middle East.

data.headline: Govt extends minimum import price on natural honey till December 31
data.summary: The Indian government has extended the minimum import price on natural honey until December 31, 2026. The minimum import price is set at USD 1400 per tonne. This move aims to protect the local apiculture industry by limiting the import of cheap honey. The extension was announced by the Directorate General of Foreign Trade. The decision is expected to support domestic honey producers and promote the growth of the apiculture sector.

data.headline: ADB raises India’s FY27 growth forecast to 6.9%
data.summary: The Asian Development Bank has raised India's economic growth forecast for FY27 to 6.9 percent. This revision is driven by increasing consumption, higher investment, and supportive government policies. The growth forecast for FY26 is projected at 7.6 percent, while FY27 anticipates a moderation due to global uncertainties. The outlook for FY28 is expected to be robust at 7 percent. India's economic growth is expected to be influenced by recent trade pacts and domestic factors.

data.headline: Global growth to slow to 3.2% in 2026, amid largest energy shock on record due to West Asia crisis: S&P Global
data.summary: Global economic growth is expected to slow to 3.2 percent in 2026. The West Asia conflict is causing the largest energy shock on record, impacting shipping, energy supply, and trade. Oil production and key infrastructure face disruptions due to the conflict. Europe and Asia, as net energy importers, will experience higher costs. The situation significantly alters the global economic outlook, with potential long-term consequences.

data.headline: 'BJP to bring UCC in 6 months': Amit Shah pitches comman civil law in Bengal
data.summary: Amit Shah has announced that the BJP plans to introduce a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) within six months. The UCC aims to establish a common set of personal laws for all Indian citizens, regardless of their religion. This code will cover aspects such as marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption, promoting equality before the law. The introduction of UCC is expected to be a highly polarizing issue in the upcoming Bengal polls. The BJP's push for UCC is in contrast to Mamata Banerjee's opposition to it, as she has fiercely resisted the idea.

data.headline: US-Iran peace talks in Pakistan: Is it happening? Lebanon hurdle puts fragile truce at risk
data.summary: Pakistan is hosting indirect US-Iran talks to stabilize a ceasefire. Iran's participation in the talks is conditional on halting Israeli strikes in Lebanon. Tehran has denied sending a delegation to the talks due to the conflict in Lebanon. The talks aim to achieve a fragile truce between the US and Iran. The success of the talks is uncertain due to the hurdle posed by the Lebanon conflict.

data.headline: Kerala student who went missing in Karnataka Chandradrona hills found dead
data.summary: A 16-year-old Kerala student, Srinandhana, was found dead in Karnataka's Chandradrona hills. She had gone missing on Tuesday near Manikyadhara Falls during a trip. Authorities conducted a search operation using thermal drones, CCTV analysis, and call records. The search involved multiple agencies and was concluded on Friday with the discovery of Srinandhana's body. The incident is a tragic outcome of the missing person's case in the Chandradrona hills.

data.headline: 'With deep anguish': What Justice Yashwant wrote in resignation letter to President
data.summary: Justice Yashwant Varma resigned from the Allahabad High Court with immediate effect. He cited deep anguish as the reason for his resignation. The resignation follows a cash recovery controversy at his residence. An ongoing impeachment process was initiated after a Supreme Court-led inquiry recommended further action. Justice Varma's resignation is a significant development in the Indian judiciary.

data.headline: 'Will be pleasing to see him in Parliament': PM Modi congratulates RS MP Nitish Kumar
data.summary: PM Modi congratulated RS MP Nitish Kumar. The occasion of the congratulations is not specified in the given text. Nitish Kumar is a member of the Rajya Sabha. PM Modi expressed his pleasure at seeing Nitish Kumar in Parliament. The statement reflects a positive relationship between PM Modi and Nitish Kumar.

data.headline: Two years after Google CEO Sundar Pichai’s ‘Code Red’, Satya Nadella orders AI overhaul
data.summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has initiated a 'Copilot code red' to enhance AI performance and user experience. The company aims to boost investor confidence amidst intense competition. Microsoft is allocating significant cloud capacity to Copilot development. Analysts suggest Azure can still exceed expectations due to growing demand and pricing. The move comes two years after Google CEO Sundar Pichai's similar 'Code Red' initiative.

data.headline: 'Forced into Ukraine war': SC seeks Centre's reply on 26 Indians stranded in Russia
data.summary: The Supreme Court has directed the Union government to respond to a habeas corpus petition regarding 26 Indian nationals stranded in Russia. These individuals were allegedly lured with job promises and forced into the Ukraine war. Their families reported that their passports were seized, and they were compelled to join military units. The court's intervention aims to secure their safe return to India. The government has been given a week to respond to the petition.

data.headline: Assembly Elections LIVE updates: BJP brought people from outside for Assam polls as it was not confident of win, says CM Mamata
data.summary: The Assembly Elections were held in Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry on April 9. A record turnout was witnessed in these states during the polling. West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee stated that the BJP brought people from outside for the Assam polls due to a lack of confidence in winning. The elections are significant as they will determine the ruling parties in these states. The high voter turnout indicates strong public participation in the democratic process.

data.headline: Lecturer beaten up on the charge of molestation in Belagavi district
data.summary: A lecturer was beaten up in Belagavi district on the charge of molestation. The incident occurred due to allegations of inappropriate behavior. The lecturer was accused by a student, leading to a physical confrontation. The police are investigating the matter and have taken statements from those involved. The incident highlights concerns about safety and accountability in educational institutions.

data.headline: Five sentenced to life imprisonment for murdering Scheduled Caste man in Madurai
data.summary: Five individuals have been sentenced to life imprisonment for murdering a Scheduled Caste man in Madurai. The murder was committed by members of a dominant intermediate caste. The victim had complained against encroachment on government poramboke land in Odaipatti, Madurai district. The incident highlights issues of caste-based violence and land disputes in the region. The sentencing aims to provide justice to the victim's family and uphold the rule of law.

data.headline: TGSRTC employees get 2.1% hike in Dearness Allowance
data.summary: TGSRTC employees have received a 2.1% hike in Dearness Allowance. The revised DA is effective from January 1, 2026. The new DA stands at 52.8%. This change was announced by Transport Minister Ponnam Prabhakar. The hike aims to adjust the employees' salaries to the current cost of living.

data.headline: BJP’s P.C. George criticises bishops, says FCRA amendment Bill will be passed
data.summary: BJP's P.C. George criticized bishops and stated that the FCRA amendment Bill will be passed. P.C. George claimed to have worked for the Church's benefit in Poonjar. He was the only candidate to do so, according to his statement. The FCRA amendment Bill is expected to have significant implications for foreign funding of NGOs and political parties. The statement has sparked controversy and debate among political and religious leaders.

data.headline: How Kerala’s Jakobi Chocolatier is rethinking chocolate, one small batch at a time
data.summary: Kerala's Jakobi Chocolatier is redefining the chocolate industry. Jacob Joy, the founder, is focusing on small-batch production. He is experimenting with unique ingredients like jackfruit and chilli-laced almonds. These custom creations are taking Indian chocolate in new directions. Jakobi Chocolatier is based in Kochi and is ditching mass production for its innovative approach.

data.headline: West Bengal SIR: SC to consider plea against ECI’s decision to freeze electoral rolls ahead of polls
data.summary: The Supreme Court will consider a plea against the Election Commission of India's decision to freeze electoral rolls. The electoral rolls were frozen on April 9 for the Assembly seats in the first stage of polls. The decision was made by the poll panel ahead of the upcoming elections. The plea is challenging the ECI's decision to finalise the electoral rolls. The Supreme Court's consideration of the plea may impact the electoral process in West Bengal.

data.headline: Kerala Assembly elections: Wayanad funds row ‘benefited’ UDF, says KPCC chief Sunny Joseph
data.summary: Kerala Assembly elections have sparked controversy over fund allocation. KPCC chief Sunny Joseph claims the Wayanad funds row benefited the UDF. He shifts focus to alleged misuse of public treasury funds and other collections. The issue involves the gold held by the Travancore Devaswom Board and Ayyappa Sangamam funds. Sunny Joseph's statement may impact the election outcome and political dynamics in Kerala.

data.headline: Protect Bengaluru traffic police from harsh summer; State Police Complaints Authority member urges immediate relief measures
data.summary: A State Police Complaints Authority member has written to the DG&IG of Police regarding the harsh summer conditions faced by Bengaluru traffic police. The member, Mohan Kumar Danappa, has urged for immediate relief measures to protect them. Traffic police personnel are forced to work under direct sunlight without access to shade, leading to exhaustion and dehydration. This situation also poses long-term health risks to the personnel. The authority member's letter aims to bring attention to the plight of traffic police and prompt action to mitigate their suffering.

data.headline: 2.49 lakh hectare crop land affected by unseasonal rains, states directed to assess damage: Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Chouhan
data.summary: Unseasonal rains have affected 2.49 lakh hectares of crop land. Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Chouhan has directed states to assess the damage. The assessment will help in providing relief to affected farmers. The move aims to mitigate the losses suffered by farmers due to the unseasonal rains. The government will provide support to farmers based on the assessment report.

data.headline: Sri Lanka’s cyclone-damaged railway lines back on track with India’s support
data.summary: Sri Lanka's cyclone-damaged railway lines have been restored with India's support. The restoration work was completed after a cyclone damaged the railway lines in the country. India provided assistance to Sri Lanka to repair the damaged railway lines. The railway lines are now operational, and train services have resumed. The support from India has helped Sri Lanka to recover from the cyclone's impact on its transportation infrastructure.

data.headline: ‘BJP can never ally with those building Babri Masjid in Bengal’: Amit Shah on Humayun Kabir row
data.summary: Amit Shah stated that the BJP cannot ally with those who support building the Babri Masjid in Bengal. This statement was made in reference to the Humayun Kabir row. Humayun Kabir is a Bengali politician who has been involved in controversy regarding his comments on the Babri Masjid. The BJP's stance on this issue highlights the ongoing tensions between different political parties in India. The statement by Amit Shah has significant implications for the political landscape in Bengal and India as a whole.

data.headline: Cash recovery row: Justice Yashwant Varma resigns amid impeachment process
data.summary: Justice Yashwant Varma has resigned from his post following a cash recovery controversy and impeachment proceedings. Allegations of unaccounted cash surfaced after a fire at his residence, prompting a Supreme Court inquiry and subsequent parliamentary action. The judge had previously questioned the constitutionality of the inquiry process. Justice Varma's resignation comes amid an ongoing impeachment process. The controversy has raised questions about judicial accountability and the independence of the judiciary.

data.headline: ‘Did nothing wrong’: Threat backlash forces Shreyas' sister to delete viral video
data.summary: Shresta Iyer, sister of India cricketer Shreyas Iyer, posted a video on social media. The video was about a washed-out match between PBKS and KKR. She received threats and her video was misunderstood, despite being light-hearted banter. Shresta Iyer clarified that her intent was supportive, but deleted the clip to prevent negativity. She urged for kindness after removing the viral video.

data.headline: Caught on cam: Motorcycle rider meets with accident during TVK chief Vijay's roadshow
data.summary: A motorcycle rider was involved in an accident during a roadshow. The roadshow was led by TVK chief Vijay. The incident was caught on camera, providing a visual record of the accident. The accident occurred during the roadshow, but the details of the rider's condition are not specified. The roadshow was likely a public event, and the accident may have caused a disruption.

data.headline: $2 tn wipeout to emergency meeting: Anthropic’s AI becomes Wall Street’s biggest headache
data.summary: US Treasury Secretary and Federal Reserve Chair held an emergency meeting with top bank CEOs to discuss Anthropic's new AI model, Claude Mythos Preview. This AI model can find software vulnerabilities, posing a significant threat to the financial system's cybersecurity. Anthropic launched Project Glasswing to address these concerns by fixing flaws before they can be exploited. The meeting was prompted by a $2 trillion wipeout, highlighting the potential impact of Anthropic's AI on the financial sector. The discussion centered around the potential risks and mitigation strategies for the financial system.

data.headline: Mukul Choudhary's untold story: Jail, loans & sacrifice - A father’s 3.5 cr gamble
data.summary: Mukul Choudhary is a 21-year-old cricketer who plays for Lucknow Super Giants. He was bought by LSG for Rs 2.60 crore. Choudhary recently helped his team pull off a win against Kolkata Knight Riders at Eden Gardens. He achieved this by smashing an unbeaten 27-ball 54, including seven sixes. Mukul Choudhary's performance made him an overnight sensation in the IPL.

data.headline: 'Tum kaale ho, mujhe deserve nahi karte': Wife plots husband murder with lover
data.summary: A man's death in Madhya Pradesh was initially reported as a robbery but was later revealed as a planned murder. The man's wife, Priyanka, allegedly conspired with her lover, Kamlesh, to kill her husband due to marital discord and humiliation. Kamlesh paid Rs 1 lakh to an accomplice for the execution. The murder was a result of the wife's unhappiness in her marriage. The case is an example of a planned murder motivated by personal relationships and financial transactions.

data.headline: Rowdy sheeters in Hyderabad test positive in surprise drug-test drive
data.summary: A surprise drug-test drive was conducted in Hyderabad. The drive targeted rowdy sheeters in the city. Several rowdy sheeters tested positive for drug use during the drive. The test results have raised concerns about the involvement of criminals in drug abuse. The drive is part of a larger effort to combat drug use and related crimes in the city.

data.headline: Tourist from Netherlands forgets guitar in train in Mysuru, RPF helps her get it back
data.summary: A tourist from the Netherlands forgot her guitar in a train in Mysuru. The Divisional Railway Manager in Mysuru reported that the tourist had expressed gratitude for the assistance. The Railway Protection Force (RPF) in Mysuru helped the tourist retrieve her guitar. The tourist appreciated the timely assistance and professionalism of the RPF. The incident highlights the efforts of the RPF in providing assistance to passengers in need.

data.headline: 15-year-old missing girl from Kerala found dead in Chikkamagaluru
data.summary: A 15-year-old girl from Kerala was reported missing. Her body was found in Chikkamagaluru. The Chikkamagaluru Superintendent of Police, Jitendra Kumar Dayama, confirmed the discovery of the body. The girl's clothes were intact when her body was found. The incident is under investigation by the local authorities.

data.headline: Justice Yashwant Varma, accused in cash-at-home scam, resigns
data.summary: Justice Yashwant Varma, an Allahabad High Court Judge, has resigned. He was accused in a cash-at-home scam. The resignation has been sent to President Droupadi Murmu. This development comes after allegations of corruption against the judge. The President will now take further action on the resignation.

data.headline: Anganwadi teacher tied to tree in Telangana village over money dispute
data.summary: An Anganwadi teacher was tied to a tree in a Telangana village. The incident occurred due to a money dispute. The teacher was allegedly involved in a financial transaction that led to the dispute. The incident has been reported to the local authorities and an investigation is underway. The incident highlights the law and order situation in the village.

data.headline: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar takes oath as Rajya Sabha member
data.summary: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar took oath as a Rajya Sabha member. The oath was administered by Rajya Sabha chairman C.P. Radhakrishnan. The ceremony was brief and held in the chairman's chamber. Nitish Kumar's oath marks his new role in the Upper House of Parliament. This development may have implications for Bihar's political landscape and national politics.

data.headline: A cafe where tyres become chairs: Hyderabad’s Samatha Kitchen reimagines waste
data.summary: Samatha Kitchen in Hyderabad has come up with a unique initiative to reuse waste materials. The cafe uses old tyres as chairs, showcasing creative ways to reduce waste. This innovative approach promotes sustainability and recycling. The cafe's initiative aims to raise awareness about the importance of reusing and recycling waste. By reimagining waste, Samatha Kitchen sets an example for environmentally friendly practices.

data.headline: Assembly Elections LIVE updates: Kannur faces post-poll violence, Congress office vandalised
data.summary: Assembly elections were held in Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry on April 9. The polling witnessed a record turnout in these states. Post-poll violence was reported in Kannur, with a Congress office being vandalised. The elections are significant for the political landscape of the country. The high voter turnout indicates strong public participation in the democratic process.

data.headline: Cyberabad police rolls out EV patrol fleet
data.summary: The Cyberabad police have introduced an electric vehicle patrol fleet. This initiative aims to reduce carbon emissions and promote sustainable policing. The fleet consists of electric vehicles that will be used for patrolling and other police duties. The introduction of EVs is expected to decrease the police department's carbon footprint. The move is part of the government's efforts to promote the use of electric vehicles and reduce pollution.

data.headline: Protect traffic police from harsh weather in Bengaluru in summer; SPCA member urges immediate relief measures
data.summary: A member of the SPCA, Mohan Kumar Danappa, has written to the DG&IG of Police in Bengaluru. The letter highlights the struggles of traffic police personnel who work under direct sunlight without access to shade. This leads to exhaustion, dehydration, and long-term health risks for the personnel. Danappa has urged for immediate relief measures to protect the traffic police from harsh weather conditions. The SPCA member's letter aims to bring attention to the welfare of traffic police personnel in Bengaluru during the summer season.

data.headline: Nitish Kumar takes oath as Rajya Sabha MP, achieves rare feat
data.summary: Nitish Kumar has taken oath as a Rajya Sabha MP. He is the first Chief Minister of Bihar to become a member of the Rajya Sabha. This achievement is considered rare as Kumar has held various positions in his political career. Kumar's oath-taking ceremony was attended by several dignitaries, including other Rajya Sabha members. His membership in the Rajya Sabha is expected to have significant implications for Bihar's political landscape.

data.headline: SC stays criminal proceedings against priest booked for saying ‘only one religion…Christian’
data.summary: The Supreme Court has stayed criminal proceedings against a priest. The priest was booked for saying only one religion is true, which is Christianity. The court's decision is based on the priest's right to freedom of speech and expression. The case highlights the balance between freedom of speech and potential hate speech. The Supreme Court's ruling may have implications for similar cases in the future.

data.headline: Marco Rubio to visit India next month, says US envoy after ‘productive meeting’ with Vikram Misri
data.summary: US Senator Marco Rubio is scheduled to visit India next month. The announcement was made by the US envoy after a meeting with Vikram Misri. The meeting between the US envoy and Vikram Misri was described as productive. The visit is expected to strengthen bilateral ties between the US and India. The exact dates and details of Senator Rubio's visit have not been disclosed yet.

data.headline: ‘BJP will implement UCC in 6 months if voted to power in Bengal’: Amit Shah
data.summary: Amit Shah stated that the BJP will implement the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in 6 months if voted to power in Bengal. The announcement was made during a public rally. The UCC aims to unify personal laws across different religions. This move is seen as an attempt to woo voters in the upcoming elections. The implementation of UCC has been a long-standing demand of the BJP.

data.headline: Debutant sparred with CM Himanta; hours after polling, her team members detained in Assam
data.summary: A debutant politician sparred with Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma. The incident occurred just hours after polling in the state. The debutant's team members were detained by the authorities. The detention has sparked controversy and raised questions about the fairness of the election process. The incident highlights the intense political rivalry in Assam and its potential impact on the state's electoral outcome.

data.headline: Justice Yashwant Varma, facing probe in cash-at-home case, resigns from Allahabad High Court
data.summary: Justice Yashwant Varma has resigned from the Allahabad High Court. He was facing a probe in a cash-at-home case. The resignation comes amid an investigation into the matter. Justice Varma's resignation has been accepted. The incident highlights concerns about judicial integrity and accountability.

data.headline: India eases rules for rice exports to some European countries
data.summary: India has eased export rules for rice, allowing basmati and non-basmati rice to be shipped to certain European nations. The exemption applies to EU member states, the United Kingdom, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and Switzerland. This rule change is for a period of six months and does not require the usual inspection certificate from India's export agency. Other European countries are already exempt from these rules. The new rule aims to facilitate rice exports to these countries.

data.headline: India eyes 20 more countries for open market access in next big trade bet
data.summary: India is negotiating with twenty additional nations for market access and trade expansion. Union Minister Piyush Goyal stated these talks build on existing free trade agreements. Discussions involve the Gulf Cooperation Council, Eurasian region, and Israel. These efforts aim to provide Indian entrepreneurs with preferential market access to two-thirds of global trade. The negotiations are part of India's next big trade bet to expand its market access globally.

data.headline: NOC denied, LSG's Rs 2 Cr star out of IPL 2026; South African player in radar
data.summary: Lucknow Super Giants' star all-rounder Wanindu Hasaranga is out of IPL 2026 due to injury. The team is considering South African spinner George Linde as a replacement. Despite Hasaranga's absence, LSG secured a thrilling last-ball victory against KKR. The win was fueled by strong performances from Ayush Badoni and Mukul Choudhary in the chase.

data.headline: Telangana HC grants one-week protection to Pawan Khera
data.summary: The Telangana High Court has granted one-week protection to Pawan Khera. This protection is from any potential arrest. Mr. Khera has been directed to approach a local court in Assam for regular bail after the one-week period. The court's decision is a temporary measure to ensure Mr. Khera's safety until he can obtain a regular bail.
